description_long: This object is a rectangular concrete jersey barrier, commonly used for traffic control and safety in urban environments. Its surface is painted with alternating diagonal yellow and black stripes, which serve as a high-visibility warning pattern. The barrier shows signs of weathering and wear, with visible scuffs and some faded paint, indicating prolonged outdoor use. The top edge is slightly beveled, and the sides taper inward toward the base, giving it a trapezoidal profile. The object appears solid and heavy, designed to prevent vehicles or pedestrians from crossing into restricted areas. There are no visible handles, openings, or interactive components, and the surface is rough and matte.
description: A solid concrete jersey barrier with yellow and black diagonal stripes, showing weathered paint and a rough, worn surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the barrier with prominent yellow and black diagonal stripes and visible surface wear.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the trapezoidal profile and the thickness of the barrier, with the striped pattern continuing along the side.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the same striped pattern and surface texture.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thickness and the continuation of the diagonal stripes.
